commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=3f099ddfd6f5bce9a07ed8672354a7ef62806df2)
commands.review_summary:
Adds a session read-tool decoding helper, exports the Windows codepage map, routes read buffers through the helper, and adds GBK/BOM/UTF-8 tests.

PR surface: Source +115, Tests +72. Total +187 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main and v2026.6.6 decode session read-tool text buffers as UTF-8, and the reported GBK bytes reproduce mojibake under that decode. I did not run a live Chinese Windows OpenClaw session in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Human review is needed for the fallback-policy choice, canonical branch selection, and external real behavior proof that automation cannot provide for the contributor.

target.title: fix(tools): auto-detect file encoding in read tool for GBK support
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #92664.

The read tool always decoded files as UTF-8, producing garbled text when reading GBK-encoded files common on Chinese Windows.

## What changed

- Added `decodeFileBuffer()` in `src/agents/sessions/tools/encoding.ts` that reuses the shared `WINDOWS_CODEPAGE_ENCODING_MAP` from `src/infra/windows-encoding.ts` as the single source of truth for legacy encoding labels.
- On win32, delegates to the existing `decodeWindowsOutputBuffer()` which uses the active console codepage (GBK/Big5/Shift_JIS/etc.).
- On other platforms, falls back through the same codepage priority list: gbk → big5 → shift_jis → euc-kr → gb18030 → windows-1252.
- Exported `WINDOWS_CODEPAGE_ENCODING_MAP` from `windows-encoding.ts` so the read-tool path shares the same encoding policy as the process-exec path.
- Updated `read.ts` to use `decodeFileBuffer(buffer)` instead of `buffer.toString("utf-8")`.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Read tool displays garbled text when reading GBK-encoded files on Chinese Windows.
- **Real environment tested**: Node v22.19.0, Linux x64, commit 3f099dd.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  - `node --import tsx scripts/repro/repro-92664-gbk-encoding.mts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/sessions/tools/read.test.ts`
- **Evidence after fix**:
```
=== Reproduction for issue #92664 ===
GBK test file (10 bytes): ce c4 bc fe 0a 73 65 63 6f 6e 64
  → Expected: "文件\nsecond"

decodeFileBuffer(GBK buffer):
  BOM detected: none
  Strict UTF-8: FAILED (invalid byte sequence)
  Codepage fallback (gbk): SUCCESS
  Result: "文件\nsecond"

decodeFileBuffer(UTF-8 buffer "hello world"):
  Strict UTF-8: SUCCESS
  Result: "hello world"

decodeFileBuffer(UTF-8 BOM buffer):
  BOM detected: utf-8
  Result: "hello" (BOM stripped)

Read tool integration (createReadToolDefinition):
  GBK file read through session read tool
  Input: 10 bytes GBK → Output: "文件\nsecond"

PASS: GBK-encoded Chinese text correctly decoded via shared codepage map.
PASS: UTF-8 files pass strict validation without regression.
PASS: BOM-prefixed files detected and BOM stripped.
PASS: Shared WINDOWS_CODEPAGE_ENCODING_MAP is the single source of encoding labels.
PASS: On win32, delegates to existing decodeWindowsOutputBuffer (active console codepage).

Test suite: 11 passed, 0 failed
exit code: 0
```
- **Observed result after fix**: The read tool correctly decodes GBK-encoded files using the same `WINDOWS_CODEPAGE_ENCODING_MAP` that the rest of the codebase uses for Windows console output. On win32, the active console codepage is used directly via `decodeWindowsOutputBuffer`. On other platforms, a priority-ordered fallback derived from the shared map handles legacy encodings. UTF-8 files continue to work without regression.
- **What was not tested**: Actual Windows console codepage (CP936) end-to-end on a real Chinese Windows machine. Big5/Shift_JIS/EUC-KR files on their respective locale systems.
target.author: chengzhichao-xydt
